name: Fig and Chocolate Tartlets
recipeInstructions:
Filling
48 semisweet chocolate chips
8 large fresh green figs
2 tablespoons red-currant jelly (melted) or
2 tablespoons strawberry jelly (melted)
Fresh mint sprigs (optional)
1. Prepare Tartlet Shells: In a food processor fitted with a chopping blade, process flour, sugar, and butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Sprinkle in water, 1 tablespoon at a time, and pulse until the pastry is moist enough to hold together when gathered into a ball. Press the pastry into a ball; divide it into 8 pieces.
2. Heat oven to 375°F (190°C). On a lightly floured surface, roll out each pastry piece to fit into a 3 1/2-by 2-inch oval tartlet pan. Trim off excess pastry. Place tartlet shells on a rimmed baking sheet. With a fork, pierce the pastry all over.
3. Bake the shells for 15 minutes or until golden brown. Remove from oven and immediately place 6 chocolate chips in the bottom of each shell; let stand 1 minute, then spread melted chocolate to coat the bottom of each shell with a butter knife. Set shells aside to cool.
4. Just before serving, prepare Filling: Cut the stem end off each fig and cut each fig lengthwise into 4 or 5 slices without cutting all the way through. Remove the shells from the pans and arrange them on a serving plate. Place one fig in each shell; fan the slices and brush with melted jelly. Garnish the plate with mint sprigs, if desired.
